rulings.Case Title: TROY SNOWDEN VS. S&S HOSPITALITY INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion To Leave To Amend And File Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 27, 2023 line 1. PLAINTIFF TROY SNOWDEN NOTICE OF MOTION, MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO LEAVE TO AMEND AND FILE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT is OFF CALENAR. Re-noticed. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 500 LEAVENWORTH APARTMENTS, LP, A CALIFORNIA VS. STEPHEN MASSEY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Dismiss Claim
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 27, 2023 line 8. DEFENDANT STEPHEN MASSEY NTC OF MTN AND MTN TO DISMISS CLAIM is DENIED. The moving party failed to identify any procedural basis for a motion to dismiss. To the extent, the moving party is asking the Court to treat this motion to dismiss as a motion to strike and demurrer (Reply 2:8-9), the request and GRANTED and the motion is taken off calendar as procedurally improper. Both the demurrer and the motion to strike are untimely in light of the answer filed on October 11, 2023. However, in light of the admission made in the opposition regarding the fictious name statement "as being executed and filed, with publication made, on November 8 2023" (opposition 3:25-28), which is contrary to the allegation of compliance with the fictitious names statutes made in the verified complaint on September 27, 2023. The Court will stay this action until Plaintiff makes a motion for leave to amend, obtains such leave and files an amended complaint accurately reflecting the state of compliance at during all relevant times. The stay does not apply to filing of the motion for leave to amend, opposition and reply thereto. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
